issues.title: uat.md issues-round Verdict line is never updated to "re-accepted" — the template's post-acceptance line has no owner
issues.description: templates/uat.template.md's issues-round format ends: '**Verdict:** re-planned — awaiting re-acceptance / (then, once accepted:) re-accepted (user, <YYYY-MM-DD>) → /gogo:go reruns ②→⑤' — the gate log is supposed to record the re-acceptance in the round itself. But no skill owns that update: gogo/SKILL.md UAT step 3 (re-acceptance) instructs the orchestrator to bump iterations uat=N and emit uat-failed, and gogo-plan's acceptance flow (SKILL.md step 6 Accept) sets state.md + plan.md Status + emits plan-accepted — NEITHER mentions appending the 're-accepted' line to the uat.md round, and agents/gogo-analyst.md step 4 explicitly STOPs before acceptance. Dogfooded live on a scratch fixture (spec-executing the skills literally through a full issues→re-accept→rerun→re-ship loop): round 1's verdict stayed 're-planned — awaiting re-acceptance' forever even after the loop re-shipped — confirmed independently on my own separate fixture dogfood of the same loop. Impact: uat.md is the contract's designated UAT gate log (cli-contract.md §1: 'verbatim input + analysis + plan delta + disposition + verdict'), and its verdict permanently misstates the round's outcome; the acceptance is only discoverable indirectly (events.jsonl uat-failed + state.md).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Give the update an owner in gogo/SKILL.md UAT step 3: after the re-acceptance lands, the orchestrator (who already bumps uat=N and emits uat-failed there) also appends the template's 're-accepted (user, <date>) → /gogo:go reruns ②→⑤' line to the round's **Verdict:** in uat.md. Mirror a one-line note in agents/gogo-analyst.md step 4 so the analyst knows not to do it itself. No template change needed — the line already exists there.
issues.fix_summary: Gave the re-accepted verdict line an owner. gogo/SKILL.md UAT step 3 now instructs the orchestrator to append the template's 're-accepted (user, <date>) → /gogo:go reruns ②→⑤' line to round N's `Verdict:` in uat.md at re-acceptance (the same step it bumps iterations uat=N / emits uat-failed). templates/uat.template.md's Verdict note and agents/gogo-analyst.md step 4 now both state the analyst leaves the round at 'awaiting re-acceptance' and STOPs, and the orchestrator appends the second line — so the round's own log records the outcome. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: h/j/k navigation keys are bound in the board but documented nowhere (help line, gogo -h, README keymap all show only arrows)
issues.description: cli/internal/tui/update.go binds 'h' (column left), 'j' (down), 'k' (up) alongside the arrows (verified: `case "left", "h":`, `case "up", "k":`, `case "down", "j":` at lines 153/159/161 and again at 242/244/246) — confirmed live in tmux (all three moved focus; 'l' is peek, NOT column-right; only the Right arrow moves columns right). But the on-screen help line, cli/main.go's static help text, and README's keymap line document only ←→/↑↓ — h/j/k appear in none of them. This predates 0.11.0 (unchanged since the board's 0.10.0 introduction) so it is NOT a regression of this feature; flagged because this feature's FR9 keymap sweep touched the help line (adding 'l peek · x del') without closing this pre-existing gap, and the now-more-complete-looking help line makes the still-missing h/j/k more conspicuous by contrast.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (or wontfix as pre-existing/out-of-scope). One-line addition in three places: the board help string (e.g. '←→/h cols · ↑↓/jk cards · …'), cli/main.go's static help, and README's keymap line. The peek viewer's own help already shows '↑↓/jk scroll' — reuse that phrasing for consistency.
issues.fix_summary: Documented h/j/k in all three keymap surfaces, reusing the peek viewer's '↑↓/jk' phrasing: board help line (cli/internal/tui/view.go → '←→/h cols · ↑↓/jk cards · …'), cli/main.go static board-keys help ('←→/h columns · ↑↓/jk cards …'), and README's keymap line ('`←→`/`h` columns · `↑↓`/`jk` cards …'). [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: This feature's own events.jsonl carries fabricated, non-monotonic timestamps, breaking the stream's documented timing signal
issues.description: events.schema.json pins ts to RFC3339 UTC and calls it 'the stream's ordering + timing signal'; cli-contract.md §5 says 'ts gives ordering; the last event is the most recent transition'. The real .gogo/work/feature-analyst-uat-and-cli-ops/events.jsonl's implement/review lines carry stamps that are suspiciously round 5-minute multiples (e.g. 05:30:00Z, 05:40:00Z, 06:00:00Z, 06:10:00Z) rather than real wall-clock stamps, and this test round's own honestly-real-time-stamped lines (02:15:52Z) sort chronologically BEFORE those fabricated 'later' lines despite being appended after them in the file — so ts-order and file-order now disagree for any consumer that sorts by ts instead of trusting file order. All lines remain schema-valid (format passes); this is a producer-discipline gap in this run's emissions, not a shipped-code bug.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (forward-looking; never rewrite history — the file is append-only). Add one line to the telemetry paragraph in gogo/SKILL.md (and/or the phase skills' event snippets): 'ts is the REAL current UTC time (shell: date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) — never an estimated or rounded time.' Optionally note in cli-contract.md §5 reader rules that file order is authoritative when ts is non-monotonic.
issues.fix_summary: Added a 'Producer discipline for ts' note to docs/cli-contract.md §5: an emitter stamps ts with the real current UTC time at emission (`date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ`) — never estimated/rounded/back-dated — so a feature's stream stays monotonic in append order; the file is append-only (never rewrite a historical line); and because emission is best-effort a reader must treat file (append) order as authoritative when ts is ever non-monotonic. Forward-looking only — the existing historical lines were left untouched (append-only). [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: The work-index classifier misclassifies a feature mid-UAT-rerun (stale report + implementing/reviewing/testing status) as ready-to-ship instead of in-progress — live-reproduced in the shipped 0.11.0 CLI
issues.description: cli-contract.md §3's classifier is first-match-wins: shipped -> ready-to-ship ('a final report exists') -> in-progress ('phase is implement/review/test'). Nothing in the pipeline clears or invalidates report/report.md when a UAT round is re-accepted and /gogo:go reruns ②→⑤ on the SAME item (checked gogo-implement/SKILL.md and gogo/SKILL.md's UAT loop in full — neither instructs archiving or deleting the prior report/ before re-entering ②). So for the entire window between re-acceptance (status flips to plan-accepted, then implementing/reviewing/testing as the rerun proceeds) and the NEXT phase-⑤ completion, report/report.md from the PRE-feedback pass is still present on disk. gogo-done/SKILL.md's own validate-in text justifies not handling this case with 'a member still mid-pipeline (implementing/reviewing/testing) has no report yet, so the report-complete check above already stops it' — a premise that is TRUE for a fresh feature but FALSE during a UAT rerun. Live-reproduced: built a fixture at .gogo/work/feature-stale-rerun/ with state.md phase=implement, status=implementing (simulating mid-rerun) plus a stale report/report.md, ran the real built 0.11.0 `gogo status` binary against it, and the output showed `CLASS=ready-to-ship PHASE=implement STATUS=implementing` for that feature — confirmed live in cli/internal/contract/contract.go's classify() (the `case f.ReportPath != "":` branch is checked and matches BEFORE the `case inProgressPhaseOrStatus(...)` branch is ever reached, exactly per the documented top-to-bottom precedence). Consequence: during a UAT rerun the board's READY column shows this card as selectable, with no waiting-for-user/awaiting-uat badge to distinguish it (badge() falls through to a plain phase-based badge like 'implement'), so a user could select and ship (`d`/`m`) a STALE, pre-UAT-feedback report while the code is actively being re-implemented — the exact 'ship an un-re-implemented plan' risk REV-004 was written to close, reachable through a different, later window in the same UAT-loop state machine that REV-004's fix does not cover (REV-004 only locks the window BEFORE re-acceptance).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE, no user decision needed. Primary fix: when gogo-implement's phase-started step (plain mode) fires on a feature whose state.md was previously awaiting-uat/done/shipped (i.e. this is a re-implementation, not a first pass), remove or move aside the stale report/ directory before proceeding, so a mid-pipeline feature never has a lingering report/report.md by construction (the classifier's existing precedence then works correctly with no contract/order change needed — no version bump). Defense-in-depth (optional): also harden cli/internal/contract/contract.go's classify() to check inProgressPhaseOrStatus() before the ReportPath check, though that changes the documented first-match-wins order in cli-contract.md §3 and would need to be called out as an additive contract clarification.
issues.fix_summary: Per decisions.md's TEST-004 note, the ready-to-ship rule now gates on a report AND a ship-gate status — not report-presence alone. cli/internal/contract/contract.go classify() rule 2 became `case f.ReportPath != "" && readyToShipStatus(f.Status):` (readyToShipStatus = status ∈ {awaiting-uat, done}); a UAT rerun's stale report with status implementing/plan-accepted/waiting-for-user now falls through to in-progress. Docs changed together: skills/gogo-status/SKILL.md rule table + awaiting-uat note, docs/cli-contract.md §3 table + note + the 0.11.0 changed-block (marked an additive clarification, no version bump). New Go test TestClassifyReadyRequiresUATStatus pins: awaiting-uat+report→ready, legacy done+report→ready, mid-rerun implementing/plan-accepted/waiting-for-user+stale report→in-progress. Status golden unaffected (its ready/legacy-ready fixtures are status=done → still ready). Chose the classifier-status-gate reading (over deleting the stale report/ at re-entry) because it is the single reading under which the UAT loop's one-legal-command property holds at the classifier layer without a version bump. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: hasLiveSession() matches session names by naive substring containment, so one feature's live session can be misattributed to a DIFFERENT feature whose slug is a substring of it — live-reproduced (wrong-session peek)
issues.description: cli/internal/tui/model.go hasLiveSession(slug, sessions) does `strings.Contains(s, slug)` with no word/hyphen-boundary anchoring. Any pair of slugs where one is a textual substring of the other's session name collides: e.g. a live session 'gogo-done-awaiting-card' (for slug 'awaiting-card') also matches slug 'waiting-card' because 'waiting-card' is literally a substring of 'awaiting-card' ('a' + 'waiting-card'). Live-reproduced: built two fixture cards (awaiting-card, waiting-card, no relation to each other), started one real tmux session named 'gogo-done-awaiting-card', and the board incorrectly showed a '● session' / 'running' live-session indicator on BOTH cards. Pressed 'l' (peek) on 'waiting-card' — the peek viewer opened showing 'peek — gogo-done-awaiting-card' and that session's actual content, i.e. a completely unrelated feature's session. This is reachable with realistic real-world slugs (not a contrived pair): e.g. 'auth'/'oauth', 'sync'/'resync', 'login'/'admin-login', 'plan'/'replan' all collide the same way. Consequence: a user checking on their own feature via peek (or worse, attach with 'a') could be shown, or attach into, a DIFFERENT feature's live claude session without any indication of the mismatch — confusing at best, and a real risk of typing commands intended for one feature into another feature's active pipeline run if attached.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE, no user decision needed. Session names follow the documented 'gogo-<action>-<sanitized-slug>' convention (launch.go sessionName()); hasLiveSession should parse each session name against that convention and compare the EXACT trailing slug component (after re-deriving the same sanitization applied when the session was created), not do a raw substring search. A minimal fix: require an exact match on the portion after the last 'gogo-<action>-' prefix, or at minimum require a non-alphanumeric boundary (start-of-string or '-') immediately before the match to rule out mid-word overlaps like 'a-waiting-card' matching 'waiting-card'. A model-level test with two fixture slugs where one is a substring of the other's session name (mirroring this exact repro) would pin it.
issues.fix_summary: Replaced the substring match with an exact boundary match owned by the launch package (which owns the naming convention): new launch.SessionMatchesSlug(session, slug) rebuilds the sanitized slug the same way sessionName does (sanitizeLabel, extracted from sessionName) and matches session == 'gogo-<action>-<sanitized>' for either action (go/done), plus uniqueSession's numeric collision suffix 'gogo-<action>-<sanitized>-<n>' (digits only). cli/internal/tui/model.go hasLiveSession and cli/internal/tui/update.go liveSessionFor now delegate to it (dropping strings.Contains; removed the now-unused strings import from update.go). session 'gogo-done-awaiting-card' no longer matches slug 'waiting-card'; 'oauth'/'auth', 'resync'/'sync' etc. no longer collide. New tests: TestSessionMatchesSlug (launch, incl. the live repro pair, the suffix cases, and 'a' vs session for 'a-b' → no match) + TestLiveSessionExactMatch (tui, model-level, incl. suffixed-session-matches-own-slug-only). [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: skills/gogo-build/SKILL.md Step 4 miscounts the wired content-file total as "9" — it is 8 once index.md (which carries no gogo:meta) is also excluded
issues.description: skills/gogo-build/SKILL.md Step 4: 'For each of the 9 content files (everything except `_discovered.md`)'. Verified directly: `.gogo/knowledge/` has 10 total .md files; excluding ONLY `_discovered.md` leaves 9 — but `index.md` is a static legend/purpose-map that carries no `gogo:meta` header (no Mode/Source/Confidence — confirmed by grep) and is never itself wired via this Step 4 Mode:proxy/owned pass. The real wired-content-file count that Step 4 actually iterates is 8 (analysis, code-review-standards, coding-rules, non-functional-requirements, project-knowledge, tech-stack, test-strategy, testing-tools). The off-by-one is a doc-arithmetic error, not a behavioral bug (Step 4's actual per-file loop presumably already skips index.md in practice, since index.md never accumulates a gogo:meta block on any real run) — but it misstates its own scope to a reader/future editor.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Change skills/gogo-build/SKILL.md Step 4's opening line to 'For each of the 8 content files (everything except `_discovered.md` and `index.md`)' to match reality.
issues.fix_summary: skills/gogo-build/SKILL.md Step 4 opening line corrected: 'For each of the 9 content files (everything except `_discovered.md`)' → 'For each of the 8 content files (everything except `_discovered.md` and `index.md`)' — index.md carries no gogo:meta and is never wired by the Step 4 proxy/owned pass. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: agents/gogo-analyst.md's external-docs-hook fallback clause omits the "record as an assumption" instruction that gogo-plan/SKILL.md's equivalent clause carries
issues.description: Both files describe the same conditional external-docs hook (FR2/analysis.md). gogo-plan/SKILL.md step 2: 'If none is available, proceed from the code + the user's description and record the external ref as an assumption for the plan.' agents/gogo-analyst.md step 2 (the same hook, condensed): '...otherwise proceed from code + the goal.' — it drops the 'record the external ref as an assumption' instruction entirely. Since gogo-analyst.md is the agent's own operating summary (gogo-plan/SKILL.md is its full manual, which it also reads), the condensed version losing this instruction is a minor fidelity gap between the two descriptions of the same procedure, not a functional contradiction (the fuller manual still carries it).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add the same clause to agents/gogo-analyst.md step 2's fallback: '...otherwise proceed from code + the goal, recording the external ref as an assumption.'
issues.fix_summary: agents/gogo-analyst.md step 2 external-docs fallback now carries the same clause as gogo-plan/SKILL.md: '...otherwise proceed from code + the goal, recording the external ref as an assumption for the plan.' [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: docs/cli-contract.md's "Changed in 0.11.0" section documents 3 of the 4 Stage C CLI additions (trash, permission mode, awaiting-uat badge) but has zero mention of FR7 (session log peek)
issues.description: Verified by grep: docs/cli-contract.md contains no occurrence of 'peek' or 'capture-pane' anywhere in the file. The frozen contract's 'Changed in 0.11.0' section explicitly calls out the launch permission-mode flag even though its own text notes that is 'Presentation/launch concern only — not part of the file read contract' — i.e. the doc's own precedent is to mention a presentation-only 0.11.0 addition anyway, for completeness, 'because it ships in the same versioned plugin bump' (the same reasoning used for the `## Custom` bullet). FR7's log-peek feature is the same kind of presentation-only, no-new-file-artifact addition, yet got no equivalent callout — an inconsistent application of the doc's own stated inclusion criterion. (Plan.md's FR6 explicitly instructs 'document it in the CLI contract' for trash; FR7 carries no equivalent explicit instruction, which may be why it was scoped out — but the doc's own precedent with permission-mode undercuts treating that omission as clearly intentional.)
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (or explicitly wontfix as intentionally out of the file-contract's scope, matching FR7's phrasing which never asked for a contract-doc mention — record that as a deliberate choice if kept). If fixed: add a short bullet to cli-contract.md's 'Changed in 0.11.0' section noting the `l` session-log-peek key (read-only tmux capture-pane, never an attach; falls back to tailing the background log for `-p` runs), mirroring the permission-mode bullet's 'presentation/launch concern only' framing.
issues.fix_summary: Added a FR7 session-log-peek bullet to docs/cli-contract.md's 'Changed in 0.11.0' section: the board's `l` key opens a read-only tmux capture-pane snapshot of a card's live session (never an attach — `a` still attaches), falling back to tailing that card's background `claude -p` log under .gogo/resources/cli/logs/; framed 'presentation/launch concern only — reads no .gogo/ contract file and writes nothing', mirroring the permission-mode bullet and 'listed here only because it ships in the same versioned plugin bump'. Now all four Stage C CLI additions (trash, permission mode, awaiting-uat badge, log peek) are called out. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: README.md's "## Agents" section never lists the new `gogo-analyst` agent this feature adds — README still describes only 4 agents
issues.description: FR2 adds a fifth agent, `agents/gogo-analyst.md` (phase ①'s specialist, mirroring developer/reviewer/tester). Confirmed directly: `agents/` contains exactly 5 files (gogo.md, gogo-analyst.md, gogo-developer.md, gogo-reviewer.md, gogo-tester.md). README.md's `## Agents` section (the user-facing catalogue of what ships) lists only `gogo`, `gogo-developer`, `gogo-reviewer`, `gogo-tester` — `gogo-analyst` does not appear anywhere in README.md (grepped the whole file, zero hits). docs/architecture.md and docs/commands.md both correctly name `gogo-analyst` in every relevant enumeration, so this is an isolated README miss, not a systemic one — but README is the primary entry point a new user reads. This is the same enumeration-sync class as REV-001 (README's knowledge-file count left out of sync), which this project's own review rated Major.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add a `gogo-analyst` bullet to README.md's `## Agents` section, e.g. between `gogo` and `gogo-developer`: "- **`gogo-analyst`** — phase ① specialist: reads `analysis.md` + the named knowledge set, analyses the goal against the actual code, and writes the plan (and re-analyses UAT feedback)."
issues.fix_summary: Added a gogo-analyst bullet to README.md's ## Agents section, between gogo and gogo-developer, mirroring the other specialists' row/blurb style: 'phase ① specialist: reads analysis.md + the named knowledge set, analyses the goal against the actual code, and writes the plan (and re-analyses UAT feedback into a plan delta on the same work item).' README now lists all five agents. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]

issues.title: templates/report.template.md's example `status:` line still shows the pre-0.11.0 value `done` for the default (clean-run) case
issues.description: templates/report.template.md line 12: `- **status:** done   <!-- a past/broken run: "report-only (incomplete run)" -->`. Since 0.11.0, gogo-knowledge/SKILL.md Step 1 sets a clean green run's report to `status: awaiting-uat` (never `done`) — the template's primary example value is now stale; only the parenthetical alternate (broken-run) case was ever documented, and it was never updated to reflect that the DEFAULT case changed too. report.md is prose (not schema-governed), so this has no functional/machine-readable impact, but an agent copying the template literally for a fresh green report would need to know to override the shown example rather than follow it as-is — the same 'no place still describes the old behaviour' class REV-003/006 fixed elsewhere in this feature, just missed in this one template.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Change line 12 to `- **status:** awaiting-uat   <!-- a past/broken run: "report-only (incomplete run)"; a legacy pre-0.11 run: "done" -->` so the shown default matches the current clean-run behaviour.
issues.fix_summary: templates/report.template.md example status line updated to the current clean-run default: '- **status:** awaiting-uat   <!-- a past/broken run: "report-only (incomplete run)"; a legacy pre-0.11 run: "done" -->'. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; TEST-004 live-reproven both directions (implementing+stale report -> in-progress; awaiting-uat -> ready); TEST-005 via the 15-case exact-match matrix]
